script:
So this happened when I was doing freelance IT work for a small company.
They didn’t have much security, just a basic server room and a couple of office PCs.
My job was simple: upgrade their backup system and make sure everything was being stored correctly.
They had this one old server that hadn’t been touched in years.
No one even remembered what it was for, but it was still running.
Naturally, I started there.
When I logged into it, I noticed something strange right away.
There were backup folders labeled by date, which is normal, but the dates didn’t make sense.
Some of them were from years ago, which was fine.
But some of them were from the future.
Like… actual future dates.
At first I assumed it was just a system clock issue.
So I checked the server time.
It was correct.
Then I opened one of the “future” backup folders.
Inside were files from the company’s main database.
Client records, invoices, internal documents.
Everything looked completely normal.
Until I opened one of the logs.
It showed activity that hadn’t happened yet.
Meetings that were scheduled for next week.
Emails that hadn’t been sent.
Even edits to files that didn’t exist yet.
That’s when I started feeling uneasy.
But I kept digging.
Because honestly, I thought maybe someone was testing something weird or messing with timestamps.
Then I found a folder dated three days ahead of the current date.
I opened it.
Everything looked like a normal backup again.
Until I found a file named “incident_report.txt”.
I clicked it.
It described a system failure that would happen in the server room.
According to the report, a power surge would corrupt multiple machines.
Including the one I was currently working on.
It even listed the exact time.
2:14 AM.
That night.
At that point I stopped thinking this was a joke.
Because the level of detail didn’t feel random.
It read like a real report written after something had already happened.
I checked the file metadata.
It said it had been created two days ago.
Which made no sense.
I decided to tell the company manager.
He laughed it off and said it was probably leftover junk from whoever set up the system years ago.
But I wasn’t convinced.
So I stayed late that night.
I don’t even know why.
Part of me just wanted to see if anything would actually happen.
Around 1:50 AM, everything was normal.
Servers humming, lights steady, nothing unusual.
At 2:10 AM, I started getting nervous.
I kept checking the clock every few seconds.
2:13 AM came.
Nothing.
I actually felt relieved for a moment.
Then at exactly 2:14 AM, the lights flickered.
Not just once.
They flickered in a pattern.
Then everything went completely dark.
All the servers shut down at the same time.
And for a few seconds, there was total silence.
When the power came back, I rushed to the old server.
It was still running.
Like nothing had happened.
So I checked the backup folders again.
There was a new one.
Dated with the current day.
Inside it was another file.
Also called “incident_report.txt”.
I opened it.
It described exactly what had just happened.
Every detail.
Even mentioned that I had stayed late to witness it.
That’s when I noticed the last line.
It said, “Next incident scheduled.”
Followed by another date.
Two days in the future.
I shut the server down after that.
I told the company the hardware was failing and needed to be replaced.
They agreed and had it removed the next week.
But before it was taken away, I copied one thing onto a USB.
That future folder.
